title: Iran claims it shot down 'F-18' over Chabahar; US fact-check tells a different story
content: US Central Command has debunked Iranian claims of shooting down an American F-16 fighter jet near Chabahar.  CENTCOM explicitly stated on X that no US fighter aircraft have been downed by Iran, refuting footage shared by Iranian state media. This denial comes as Iran rejected a US ceasefire proposal, insisting on its own terms for ending hostilities.

title: If they take any step forward ... ': Iran claims enemies preparing to occupy its island
content: Iran warns of potential island seizure by "enemy countries" with regional support, vowing "relentless" strikes on vital infrastructure. This comes amid a significant US military buildup in the Middle East, with analysts suggesting the UAE might be involved and Kharg Island as a potential target.

title: 'Thought of jumping from car': Sivaramakrishnan on how racism scars led to depression
content: Sivaramakrishnan, who played 25 international matches for India in a five-year career, said the struggles became severe during the IPL held in the UAE during the Covid-19 lockdown. “I was down completely and I didn’t want to see myself in the mirror. I would have a couple of drinks and go to sleep because I could not bear anything. Whenever I was awake, I thought I was going to die.”

title: Meta cuts hundreds of jobs, goes in line with Zuckerberg's previous statement
content: Meta has initiated layoffs, impacting hundreds of employees globally as part of a significant reorganization. This move follows earlier cost-cutting measures and a focus on AI investments. CEO Mark Zuckerberg highlighted AI's transformative potential, suggesting increased efficiency and a shift towards individual talent driving projects. The company aims to offset rising AI expenses and compensation costs.

title: Prithvi Shaw seeks redemption: ‘Humans make mistakes but that’s history’
content: Returning to the IPL after a year's absence, Prithvi Shaw appears more focused and mentally stronger, having learned from past excesses. The break from cricket, coupled with personal reflection, has allowed him to rebuild his confidence and dedication, as he now prioritises his passion for the game and harbours hopes of an India comeback.

title: Op Urja Suraksha: Navy deploys 5 warships to guide cargo vessels exiting troubled Strait of Hormuz
content: India's Navy has launched Operation Urja Suraksha.  More than five frontline warships are ensuring safe passage for India-bound cargo ships.  These vessels carry vital supplies like LPG and crude oil.  The operation escorts ships out of the troubled Strait of Hormuz.  This protects critical maritime routes and Indian crew.  Key successes have already been achieved.

title: ‘Monsters’: BJP leader’s son held for alleged sexual abuse of minors in Goa
content: Goa BJP councillor's son, Soham Naik, arrested for allegedly sexually exploiting minors, recording videos, and circulating them online. Investigations reveal incidents occurred when both accused and survivors were minors. The case has been transferred to the state crime branch, with Naik remanded to four-day police custody.

title: Billionaire founder Mark Cuban makes AI prediction employees may like
content: Mark Cuban predicts a future where AI adoption allows companies to shorten workdays by an hour, maintaining current pay. He urges businesses to embrace AI immediately, warning that those who don't will falter. Cuban emphasizes understanding AI's capabilities and risks, likening its memory to a savant but noting its lack of true understanding.

title: 'Don't repeat Rohit mistake': Ex-cricketer urges MI to stick with Hardik amid Surya captaincy noise
content: Mumbai Indians have several experienced players in the dressing room, including Rohit Sharma and Suryakumar Yadav, both of whom have led India in T20 cricket. A couple of seasons ago, the franchise replaced Rohit with Hardik as captain, ending a tenure that brought five IPL titles. The decision drew reactions from sections of fans, especially with Hardik moving from Gujarat Titans and taking charge immediately.

title: Are banks open or closed today on Ram Navmi? Chek state-wise list
content: Bank customers must check holiday schedules before visiting branches this week. Closures will occur due to festivals and state holidays.  RBI guidelines also mean branches will be shut on the second and fourth Saturdays of March.  Plan your banking needs accordingly. Digital platforms like mobile apps and UPI remain active for everyday transactions.  Services requiring branch visits will be unavailable.

title: Musk wants a judge removed from all cases because she liked a LinkedIn post
content: Elon Musk's legal team is seeking the removal of Delaware Chancellor Kathaleen McCormick from his company cases, alleging bias after she 'liked' a LinkedIn post celebrating his recent legal defeat. McCormick stated she was unaware of the 'like' and may have done so accidentally, though she expressed doubt about this possibility.

title: GT playing XI for IPL 2026: Gill-Sudharsan to open, Holder vs Phillips in focus
content: Gujarat Titans enter IPL 2026 with continuity, retaining 20 players. After a third-place finish last season, they aim to refine their squad, adding five new faces including Jason Holder and Tom Banton. With a strong core featuring Sai Sudharsan and Rashid Khan, GT is poised for another playoff run.

title: 12 lakh Bengal voter claims rejected out of 32L vetted: EC official
content: Bengal's electoral rolls saw a significant reduction of approximately 12 lakh voters after judicial officers cleared just over half of the 60 lakh pending cases. This has led to widespread disenfranchisement in several rural pockets, with many long-term residents finding their names deleted despite submitting documentation.

title: ‘Dhurandhar: The Revenge’ box office: Ranveer Singh's film inches close to Rs 1,000 crore
content: Despite a significant dip after its opening week, 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' has achieved a remarkable box office feat, nearing Rs 1000 crore globally within just seven days. The spy thriller, starring Ranveer Singh, experienced a massive opening weekend, breaking preview records before facing a decline in collections.

title: A’ja Wilson candidly recounts meeting with her fashion role model Beyoncé at Formula 1 race
content: A’ja Wilson reflected on meeting Beyoncé at a Formula 1 event, calling it a moment where “Nothing else matters.” The Las Vegas Aces star continues to grow her influence beyond basketball, blending fashion with elite performance. While embracing style and major platforms like the Met Gala, Wilson remains focused on her game, balancing off-court presence with her dominance and achievements on the court.

title: Candace Owens defends his Erika Kirk remarks; debate over free speech grows
content: Candace Owens supported Joe Rogan after his comments about Erika Kirk resurfaced online. She said Rogan speaks what people already think, which is why he stays popular. The issue started from an old video where Kirk discussed serious topics. Now, the debate has shifted to free speech, public opinion, and whether such comments go too far.

title: Philadelphia Eagles add $5M wide receiver to help Jalen Hurts elevate passing game
content: The Philadelphia Eagles have signed Elijah Moore to bolster their passing attack, aiming to provide Jalen Hurts with another reliable target. Moore's speed and ability to create separation are expected to ease pressure on top receivers and improve offensive rhythm. Despite a down year in 2025, his previous production and college connection with A.J.  if not, the pressure will remain on Jalen Hurts to elevate the offence. Either way, the Eagles have made it clear. They expect their passing game to improve in

title: Is Ram Navami on March 26 or 27? check correct date, time and significance
content: Ram Navami, celebrating the birth of Lord Shri Ram, the seventh incarnation of Lord Vishnu, will be observed on March 26, 2026. This prominent Hindu festival marks the end of Chaitra Navratri and is celebrated with prayers, recitations of Ramacharitmanas, and acts of charity, including Kanya Pujan.

title: Stock market holiday on March 26: Will NSE, BSE remain shut for trading on Ram Navmi?
content: Investors can note that Dalal Street will observe a holiday on March 26 for Ram Navmi, as per the exchange's official calendar. This early pause in the trading week means equity markets will be closed. The article also lists upcoming stock market holidays for 2026, including Shri Mahavir Jayanti and Good Friday.

title: 'What's wrong?': David Warner loses cool at PSL captains’ presser - Watch
content: PSL 2026 faces multiple issues, including a fuel crisis and venue changes. During a captains’ press conference, David Warner showed frustration at others talking, saying, “What’s wrong? Sorry gentlemen, we have got school kids here.” PCB confirmed travel restrictions and cancelled events due to the crisis, with matches now limited to Lahore and Karachi.

title: UK court rejects Nirav Modi's bid to stall extradition
content: A UK court has dismissed fugitive diamantaire Nirav Modi's plea to reopen his extradition order, bringing him closer to facing charges in India for a Rs 6,500 crore Punjab National Bank scam.  The High Court found no exceptional circumstances to revisit the earlier decision, despite Modi's concerns about ill-treatment.  This development follows years of legal proceedings since his 2019 arrest.

title: Happy Ram Navami 2026: Best messages, quotes, wishes, and images to share
content: Ram Navami offers a unique, grounded morning, marked by quiet traditions and the enduring values of Lord Ram: balance, righteousness, and patience. Beyond rituals, the day emphasizes simple acts of connection, like sending wishes. The article provides heartfelt, thoughtful, and family-oriented messages, alongside concise lines for social media, encouraging a moment of reflection and peace amidst daily life.

title: Space co Agnikul successfully tests 3D-printed ‘Agnite’ booster engine
content: Indian space company Agnikul Cosmos has successfully tested its 3D-printed booster engine, 'Agnite'. This single-piece Inconel engine, a meter long, can be fully printed within a week, significantly reducing production complexity and turnaround time. The successful in-house testing marks a crucial end-to-end cycle completion for their larger engines.

title: 'Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone' teaser: Harry, Ron, Hermione return to Hogwarts
content: A fresh Harry Potter era dawns as HBO unveils a teaser for its 'Philosopher's Stone' series, introducing new actors like Dominic McLaughlin as Harry. The trailer revisits iconic moments from Harry's early life and his arrival at Hogwarts. This reimagining promises a more detailed exploration of J.K. Rowling's beloved saga, set to premiere this Christmas on HBO Max.

title: PM Modi remains world's most popular leader, says study by US firm
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has once again been recognized as the world's most popular democratic leader, achieving a 68% approval rating in a recent Morning Consult survey. This significant endorsement highlights his strong public support, even as it shows a slight decrease from last year's figures. Modi continues to lead global leaders by a considerable margin.

title: BRO entrusted with task of developing infra along 1600-km Myanmar border: Rajnath at parl panel meet
content: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh announced that the Border Roads Organisation (BRO) will develop infrastructure along the 1,600 km Indo-Myanmar border to bolster management capabilities. The BRO is also ensuring all-weather connectivity in remote regions and adopting modern construction techniques to enhance operational mobility and defence preparedness.

title: Middle East crisis: Amit Shah heading informal GoM on 'overall strategic issues'
content: Home Minister Amit Shah is leading an informal Group of Ministers to address strategic issues arising from the West Asia conflict, including impacts on cooking gas and LNG supplies. Another GoM, headed by Manohar Lal, is focusing on energy supply matters, particularly piped gas for homes, as the government prioritizes PNG adoption.

title: Precision rockets redefine war: Why India’s Pinaka push now matters more
content: America's Central Command reports a record artillery strike in Iran using the Precision Strike Missile. This advanced weapon system demonstrates significant reach. India is also enhancing its rocket artillery, including the Pinaka system, to extend its strike capabilities. Modern warfare increasingly relies on such versatile rocket systems for precision targeting and strategic advantage.

title: 'LPG charge' not legal, will invite action, CCPA warns eateries
content: Hotels and restaurants face strict action for levying additional charges like "LPG charges" or "fuel cost recovery" on food bills. The Central Consumer Protection Authority (CCPA) stated these are unfair trade practices, as operational costs should be included in menu prices. Consumers can dispute such charges and file complaints.

title: Have enough fuel stocks, don't believe rumours, says govt
content: Government and major oil companies have strongly refuted rumors of petrol and diesel shortages, urging the public to avoid panic buying. Officials confirmed ample fuel stocks nationwide and assured citizens that there are no impending price hikes.

title: With Rs 1,800cr outlay, immigration scheme extended for 5 years
content: The Union cabinet has approved the continuation of the immigration and visa tracking scheme for five more years until March 2031 with a Rs 1,800 crore outlay. This initiative aims to enhance national security and streamline the visa and immigration process for foreign visitors by inter-linking multiple databases for informed decision-making.

title: Global boost: IITs make mark in QS top 50 rankings by subject
content: Indian Institutes of Technology are solidifying their global standing in engineering, with multiple disciplines ranking in the world's top 50. Management education is also advancing, as seen with IIM Ahmedabad's rise. Overall, India's higher education system shows significant quality improvement and global competitiveness.

title: Have PNG access but not switched? Govt will stop LPG refills
content: Households with access to piped natural gas but still using LPG cylinders will face a supply cut in three months. This government move aims to reduce LPG imports, with exceptions for technically infeasible PNG connections. City gas firms will notify eligible consumers, while authorities must now grant pipeline permissions within set deadlines.

title: Financial burden no excuse to deny fair dues for land buys: SC
content: The Supreme Court ruled that financial burden cannot deny fair compensation for land acquisition, upholding the principle that fiscal implications don't override landowners' entitlements. However, the court limited this liberal compensation regime to claims pending since March 28, 2008, preventing delayed appeals for enhanced payouts.

title: Amid oppn protest, FCRA amendment bill tabled in Lok Sabha
content: The government introduced the Foreign Contribution (Regulation) Amendment Bill, 2026, in Lok Sabha, facing opposition from Congress and TMC who voiced concerns over executive overreach. Minister of State for Home Nityanand Rai countered, stating the bill targets misuse of foreign funds for conversions and personal gain, aiming to enhance transparency in NGO funding for public welfare.

title: 'It's not must': SC nixes plea against Vande Mataram circular
content: Raza Academy president Muhammed Sayed Noori has challenged the Centre's order for schools to begin the day with the national song 'Vande Mataram' in the Supreme Court. He argued that compelling students to sing the song, which invokes Hindu deities, infringes upon religious freedom and secularism.

title: 'Trump won’t dictate war end’: Iran rebuffs US 15‑point proposal, sets 5 demands
content: Iran has rejected a US peace proposal, stating it will end the conflict only on its own terms and timeline. Tehran demands a halt to attacks, guarantees against future wars, war damage compensation, an end to regional hostilities, and sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Dead ball! Umpire pulls up Sri Lankan spinner for copying Usman Tariq action - Watch
content: Tariq’s action, which includes a visible halt before delivering the ball, has already come under scrutiny, with indirect suggestions of chucking from Australia all-rounder Cameron Green.

title: Brown-led Celts hand Thunder first loss in month
content: Jaylen Brown scored 14 of his 31 points in a pivotal third quarter, and the Boston Celtics rallied to beat the Oklahoma City Thunder 119-109 on Wednesday night, snapping the defending champions' 12-game win streak.

title: Nacua sued over alleged antisemitic remark, bite
content: A woman in Los Angeles has filed a civil lawsuit against Puka Nacua alleging the Rams wide receiver made an "unprovoked antisemitic statement" and later bit her on the shoulder.

title: Arizona's Lloyd brushes off UNC job speculation
content: Amid speculation he could be a candidate for the vacancy at North Carolina, Arizona coach Tommy Lloyd insisted all his attention is with his program ahead of Thursday's Sweet 16 game against Arkansas.

title: Macdonald: JSN deal rewards 'foundational' player
content: Seahawks GM John Schneider and coach Mike Macdonald said they felt comfortable giving Jaxon Smith-Njigba his record-setting extension because of his "unique combination" of excellence on and off the field.

title: Flacco: Teams 'dumb' for not signing me to start
content: Joe Flacco is back with the Bengals as Joe Burrow's backup, but he isn't shy about voicing his belief that teams made a mistake by passing on him to be their starting quarterback.

title: Seahawks to open on Wed. due to Australia game
content: The Super Bowl LX champion Seahawks will open the season on the Wednesday of Week 1 instead of hosting the usual Thursday night opener.

title: Padres' Darvish to restricted list amid elbow rehab
content: Pitcher Yu Darvish was placed on the restricted list Wednesday as he spends the 2026 season recovering from elbow surgery, a decision that could free up significant payroll space for the Padres.

title: Mahomes shows progress, posts video throwing
content: Patrick Mahomes posted a short video clip Wednesday of himself in a training session where he dropped back and threw the ball, the first public footage of him doing so since he had knee surgery in December.

content: A'ja Wilson will likely get the $1.4 million supermax, while Caitlin Clark's rookie contract will pay over $500,000.

content: It was a huge night for Manchester United in the UEFA Women's Champions League, but they will need to bounce back next week to make it even more historic.

title: Forget Ronaldo, Rooney or Henry: Salah is the Premier League's greatest-ever player
content: Cristiano Ronaldo has five Ballon d'Ors and Thierry Henry will forever be an "Invincible," but neither had a Premier League career to match Mohamed Salah's.

title: Cost of the World Cup: How much might a USMNT fan spend to follow their team?
content: From tickets to hotels, how expensive is this summer's big event? Let's find out -- through the eyes of a USMNT fan on the trip of a lifetime.

content: The final six spots -- four from Europe and two from the intercontinental playoff -- will be clinched this week.

content: Azzi Fudd's "imperfectly perfect" family has been her rock through ACL tears, dog food pranks and Beyoncé on repeat. Now they're hoping for one more NCAA tournament title for UConn.

title: LPG supply to be cut off if households refuse PNG switch where available: Government order
content: As India grapples with an LPG shortage due to the war in West Asia disrupting supplies from key sources, the government is pushing households and commercial users to switch to Piped Natural Gas (PNG)

title: Madras HC dismisses S.P. Velumani’s petition against I-T assessment order on 2016 Assembly poll expenses
content: Justice C. Saravanan rejects the 2023 writ petition filed against an assessment order passed in 2022 by treating the ₹7 crore as unexplained expenditure

title: BJP fields R.G. Kar victim’s mother from Panihati in West Bengal Assembly polls
content: A few days ago, Abhaya’s mother had confirmed that she will contest from Panihati Assembly seat on a BJP ticket. Several BJP leaders including Leader of Opposition Suvendu Adhikari had reached out to the family convincing the mother to contest the polls

title: No refund for last minute cancellation: Railways introduces new ticketing reforms for passengers
content: Union Minister for Railways Ashwini Vaishnaw said the changes are aimed at curbing misuse of the ticketing system and improving access for genuine passengers

content: “As I listened to them, one thing became immediately clear: their frustration is not just about a ‘project’. This is about people, their identity, and their right to live with dignity on their own land,” Mr. Gandhi said.

title: T.N. election 2026: Encroachments, traffic congestion remain core issues in Anna Nagar
content: Unregulated parking is one of the major issues faced by Anna Nagar residents; they say industrial effluents are being discharged into Otteri Nullah waterway, leading to groundwater contamination and demand the shifting of all mobile eateries to non-residential areas in the constituency

title: India’s ‘newest voters’ caught in SIR adjudication trap in West Bengal
content: Thousands relocating from erstwhile enclaves got citizenship after 2015 Land Boundary Agreement, but have now been put “under adjudication” after SIR; angry voters claim this was done along communal lines

title: Ignoring Opposition’s notices, Rajya Sabha debates CAPF Bill
content: Bill aims to regulate recruitment, deputation, promotion, and other service conditions for paramilitary officers.

content: Papa Rao was also known by the aliases Ashok, Sunnam Chandrayya, and Mangu, and his association with the Maoist movement dated back to 1997.

title: China border patrols went up significantly post Galwan clash, data shows
content: From 173 patrols per month in 2017-18, the ITBP has ramped up the number of patrols to around 500 in 2023-24; the data was reported in the Union Home Ministry’s annual report for 2024-25 which was published on Wednesday

title: Parliament clears Transgender Bill amid an uproar
content: The Opposition demanded the Bill be sent to a select committee of the House for scrutiny; Union Minister says it will ensure protection to persons who face discrimination due to biological issues

content: The “non-aligned” entities include All India United Democratic Front, United People’s Party Liberation, and All India Trinamool Congress

content: Several videos surfaced online showing long vehicle queues in various districts like Indore, Ujjain, Mandsaur, Shajapur and Jhabua

content: Southern representative Kalki Subramaniam and North East representative Rituparna Neog called the amendment Bill “a step backward for our fundamental rights to self-identification and dignity”

content: The High Court observed that the nature of the incidents was “akin to a genocide against a particular community”

title: After GHADC amendment, Meghalaya groups seek curbs on non-tribal voters
content: The demand follows the Governor’s approval of an amendment making it compulsory for candidates contesting the council elections to possess a Scheduled Tribe certificate

content: Ashok Choudhary, a close aide of Chief Minister Nitish Kumar, was selected for the post of Assistant Professor after he cleared the Bihar State University Service Commission recruitment exam 2020 in Political Science.

title: Office commute times nearly identical in Bengaluru, Hyderabad: report
content: Data from MoveInSync indicates that the average one-way commute is about 59 minutes in Bengaluru and 58 minutes in Hyderabad, suggesting similar overall travel burdens

title: Allahabad HC issues notice to Union government, state and ASI on dilapidated condition of various heritage sites in U.P.
content: The court was hearing a public interest litigation filed by lawyer Akash Vashishtha on the condition of heritage structures at various places, including Jhansi, Vrindavan, Agra, Lucknow and Hastinapur

title: Jaishankar defends India’s stand as Opposition targets govt. on ‘Pak. mediation’ in West Asia
content: At all‑party meet on West Asia crisis, Opposition questions India’s silence, PM’s Tel Aviv visit, and Pakistan’s growing diplomatic role.

content: According to the ED,  the Mahadev Online Book operated as a large-scale international betting syndicate, which facilitated illegal betting through multiple online platforms and domain names

content: Launching his campaign in Mylapore, AIADMK leader says NDA will form the government with an absolute majority; accuses DMK of not fulfilling poll promises and failing on law and order front

content: The layoffs will affect employees both in the US and other international markets, according to a person familiar with the matter.

title: JD Vance May Travel To Pakistan For Iran War Talks Over Weekend: Report
content: Media reports have claimed that Iranian negotiators have refused to sit down with Trump's Middle East envoy, Steve Witkoff, or Trump's son-in-law Jared Kushner, who led the nuclear negotiations with Iran before the war.

title: Railways Fines Caterer Rs 10 Lakh Over Passenger Food Complaint On Vande Bharat
content: A passenger's complaint regarding the quality of food found on train number 21896 (Patna-Tatanagar Vande Bharat Express) on March 15 has been taken seriously, and a penalty of Rs 10 lakh has been imposed on IRCTC.

content: Iran has also claimed responsibility for damaging a US F-35 stealth fighter, which made an emergency landing at an American base in the Middle East after sustaining suspected fire during a mission over Iranian airspace.

content: A candidate from a party pushing to fast-track Greenland's independence will soon represent the island in the Danish parliament.

content: Zubeen Garg, a cultural icon of Assam, had travelled to Singapore to attend the North East India Festival and died on September 19, 2025.

content: Stock Market Closed Today: Overall, 2026 has 16 scheduled market holidays. Three have already passed.

content: A comparison of NASA's Black Marble nighttime satellite imagery reveals a large part of the country going dark on March 22, compared with the same date last year.

title: India Raises Climate Ambition: 47% Emissions Cut, 60% Clean Energy By 2035
content: Government data shows that between 2005 and 2020, India reduced emissions intensity by 36%, crossed 52% non-fossil fuel capacity in the power mix, and created a 2.3 billion-tonne carbon sink.

content: Swiss Islamic scholar Tariq Ramadan was sentenced on Wednesday to 18 years in prison by a Paris criminal court for the rape of three women.

title: 16 Killed, Many Missing As Bus Falls Into River In Bangladesh
